nodes.name: Sulawesi blue flycatcher
nodes.summary: The Sulawesi blue flycatcher (Cyornis omissus) is a species of bird in the family Muscicapidae. It is endemic to Indonesia. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests and subtropical or tropical moist montane forests. Four subspecies are recognised: C. o. omissus (Hartert, EJO, 1896) – montane Sulawesi C. o. omississimus Rheindt, Prawiradilaga, Ashari & Suparno & Gwee, 2020 – Togian Islands (between northeast and central east Sulawesi) C. o. peromissus Hartert, EJO, 1920 – Salayar (south of Sulawesi) C. o. djampeanus (Hartert, EJO, 1896) – Tanahjampea (south of Sulawesi) The subspecies C. o. djampeanus is sometimes recognised as a separate species, the Tanahjampea blue flycatcher. References
nodes.shortSummary: The Sulawesi blue flycatcher (Cyornis omissus) is a species of bird in the family Muscicapidae. It is endemic to Indonesia.
